title: NOC 31100 – Specialists in Clinical & Laboratory Medicine | GTR Immigration
description: Medical specialists are among the highest-demand and highest-paid professionals in Canada’s healthcare system. As hospitals expand capacity and provinces address physician shortages, opportunities for internationally trained specialists continue to grow. Classified under NOC 31100 (TEER 1), this occupation aligns with:
duties: Diagnose and treat complex diseases and disorders
Order and interpret laboratory tests and imaging
employment_requirements: Graduation from an approved medical school
Successful certification with the Royal College of Physicians and Surgeons of Canada
Provincial licensing through relevant medical regulatory authority
Without provincial licensure, specialists cannot practice independently in Canada.
job_outlook: This indicates continued demand, particularly for:
🩺 Planning Canada PR as a Medical Specialist?
GTR Immigration can evaluate your eligibility under NOC 31100, guide you through licensing requirements, and identify the most strategic immigration pathway into Canada’s healthcare system.
additional_information: Acute-care hospitalsSpecialty medical clinicsCancer centers & diagnostic labsRehabilitation facilitiesTeaching hospitals & research institutions
Typical annual earnings: $100,700–$557,400 CAD, depending on specialization, province, and practice model.
Specialties such as radiology, anesthesiology, oncology, and pathology are particularly well compensated.
